reason: The market is exhibiting strong bearish signals. Bitcoin has failed to hold critical psychological levels ($70,000 and $68,000) despite recent positive news, indicating weak buyer conviction and significant selling pressure. The strengthening dollar suggests macro headwinds, while an investment firm's prediction of a further 30% crash highlights substantial downside risk. Critically, the news of a BlackRock private credit fund facing issues and impacting crypto prices signals potential institutional deleveraging and broader market instability. These factors combined point to a highly precarious environment with elevated risk; thus, a cautious 'WATCH' approach is prudent.
headlines: Bitcoin slips below $68,000 heading into the weekend as dollar posts steepest weekly gain in a year
Bitcoin could crash by another 30% as four-year cycle gains strength, investment firm says
Why bitcoin couldn't hold $70,000 despite its best week of Wall Street news in months
Managing financial AI agents is the only skill you’ll need to survive the AI layoffs
BlackRock private credit fund is latest to crack, hitting crypto prices and DeFi markets
